summary.intent: Merge main into the mcp-timeout-config branch, resolve conflicts, then address all PR review comments from Greptile and human reviewers on PR #419.
summary.outcome: All merge conflicts resolved, all 8 human review comments addressed, Greptile issues fixed (is_alive 2xx check, field rename, TOCTOU reordering), and build/lint errors from the merge repaired. Branch pushed and up to date.

repo: Clippy is configured with `-D clippy::expect_used` and `-D clippy::unwrap_used` project-wide; tests needing panics must use `#[allow(clippy::expect_used)]` on the test function.
Biome is used for JS formatting and rustfmt for Rust; both are enforced in CI via `cargo fmt --check` and biome check.
McpServerEntry struct requires `request_timeout_secs` field — new test entries added via main merge were missing it.
The project uses git worktrees under ~/.superset/worktrees/moltis/ for branch isolation.
Greptile code review is integrated into the PR flow and scores PRs; re-triggering after fixes may review an older merge commit rather than HEAD, causing stale scores.

code.finding: SseTransport::with_auth_remote call needed rustfmt line-wrapping to pass format check.
TOCTOU bug: disk write was happening before in-memory AtomicU64 update. Fixed by reordering — update memory first (infallible), then persist to disk.
Test used .expect() which is forbidden by clippy::expect_used lint. Fixed with #[allow(clippy::expect_used)] on the test function.
is_alive() was returning false for valid Streamable HTTP MCP servers because it checked for text/event-stream content-type. Fixed to return true for any 2xx response.
Field effective_request_timeout_secs renamed to configured_request_timeout_secs to clarify it reflects the configured value applied on next connect, not the currently active timeout.

workflow: Greptile re-review may target a merge commit rather than the branch HEAD, making its score appear stale even after fixes are pushed.
Running `cargo check` on individual crates (-p flag) is faster than full workspace checks for verifying targeted fixes.
Format failures from biome and rustfmt are separate CI jobs and must both be checked after any JS or Rust edits.

friction: Greptile reviewed a merge commit instead of HEAD after re-trigger, causing confusion about whether the is_alive fix was actually applied.
The main merge introduced new McpServerEntry usages in doctor_commands.rs that were missing the required request_timeout_secs field, causing build failures discovered only after pushing.
The .expect() in a test was caught by clippy only during the full lint run, not during earlier cargo check passes.
Multiple round-trips needed to determine which Greptile comments were stale vs. genuinely unaddressed.
open_items: Greptile score is still based on a pre-fix commit; the PR score (3/5) may not update until another review cycle is triggered or a new commit is pushed.
The mcp.config.get reading from disk on every call (noted as informational in the review) was not addressed — UI shows a restart caveat but the underlying read-on-every-call behavior remains.
